blocks.body: Need / Needn't / Don't have to helps you talk about necessity and lack of necessity without confusing them with prohibition.

{"level":"A2-B1","intro":"In this topic you will see how to talk about necessity and lack of necessity with need to, needn't, and don't have to.","rules":[{"label":"NECESSITY","color":"emerald","text":"Need to shows that something is necessary.","example":"I need to finish this report."},{"label":"NO NECESSITY","color":"blue","text":"Needn't and don't have to show that an action is not necessary.","example":"You needn't wait. / You don't have to wait."},{"label":"NOT THE SAME AS MUSTN'T","color":"amber","text":"Mustn't = not allowed. Needn't / don't have to = not necessary.","example":"You mustn't touch it. / You don't have to touch it."}]}

{"title":"1. Key forms","intro":"Need to behaves like an ordinary verb, while needn't works like a modal-like negative without to after it.","items":[{"label":"Necessity","title":"need to / needs to + V","subtitle":"need to leave / needs to study"},{"label":"No necessity","title":"don't / doesn't need to + V","subtitle":"don't need to hurry"},{"label":"Modal-like negative","title":"needn't + V","subtitle":"needn't wait / needn't worry"},{"label":"Common alternative","title":"don't have to + V","subtitle":"don't have to come early"}]}

{"title":"2. Main uses","sections":[{"label":"Need to = necessity","color":"emerald","description":"Need to shows that an action is necessary or practically required.","examples":[{"en":"I need to charge my phone.","ua":"I need to charge my phone."},{"en":"She needs to speak to the manager.","ua":"She needs to speak to the manager."},{"en":"Do we need to book a table?","ua":"Do we need to book a table?"}]},{"label":"Needn't and don't have to","color":"blue","description":"Both forms show lack of necessity. In many situations, they are very close in meaning.","examples":[{"en":"You needn't bring any food. I've already cooked.","ua":"You needn't bring any food. I've already cooked."},{"en":"You don't have to come early tomorrow.","ua":"You don't have to come early tomorrow."},{"en":"We needn't rush. There is plenty of time.","ua":"We needn't rush. There is plenty of time."}],"note":"Needn't often sounds like a short modal-like form; don't have to is very common in neutral everyday speech."},{"label":"Do not mix it up with mustn't","color":"amber","description":"If the action is forbidden, use mustn't. If it is simply optional, use needn't or don't have to.","examples":[{"en":"You mustn't park here.","ua":"You mustn't park here."},{"en":"You don't have to drive. We can walk.","ua":"You don't have to drive. We can walk."},{"en":"You needn't wash the floor today.","ua":"You needn't wash the floor today."}],"note":"This is a key difference: prohibition and no necessity are not the same thing."}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using to after needn't.","wrong":"She needn't to come.","right":"✅ She needn't come.","hint":"After needn't, use the bare infinitive."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using mustn't when you only mean there is no necessity.","wrong":"You mustn't bring food. I already ordered.","right":"✅ You don't have to bring food. I already ordered.","hint":"If something is optional, that is not a prohibition."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Forgetting the third-person form needs to.","wrong":"He need go now.","right":"✅ He needs to go now.","hint":"Need to behaves like an ordinary verb: he needs to...."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick rule","items":["Need to = it is necessary.","Needn't = it is not necessary.","Don't have to = also not necessary, and it is a very common neutral option.","Mustn't does not mean the same thing: it expresses prohibition, not lack of obligation.","After needn't, use the base verb, while need to changes like an ordinary verb."]}
